title: Tools & Software
text: All tools are freely available under permissive licences. Source code is on GitHub.

title: Tools & Software
text:
Prediction

  
    
      
      DeepLC
    
    Predicts when peptides will elute from the LC column during a mass spectrometry run. Unlike other tools, it handles modified peptides accurately, even modifications it has never seen before. When applied to a new instrument or experimental setup, it adapts quickly through transfer learning, requiring only a small number of example peptides rather than a full retraining.
    
       GitHub
       Docs
       PyPI
       Bioconda
       Web app
       Paper 2021
       Paper 2026
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Robbin Bouwmeester
     Ralf Gabriels
  


  
    
      
      MS²PIP
    
    Predicts fragment ion peak intensities from peptide sequence alone. These predictions are used to build in silico spectral libraries and to power the MS²Rescore rescoring pipeline. Fine-tuning for user-defined modifications is possible through MS²DIP.
    
       GitHub
       Web app
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Arthur Declercq
     Ralf Gabriels
  


  
    
      
      IM2Deep
    
    Predicts collisional cross-section (CCS) values for peptides including multiply modified ones. Supports ion mobility filtering in DDA and DIA workflows.
    
       GitHub
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Arthur Declercq
     Robbin Bouwmeester

title: Tools & Software
text:
Rescoring

  
    
      
      MS²Rescore
    
    Rescores peptide-spectrum matches by combining predicted LC-MS/MS features (retention time, fragment intensities, and ion mobility) with observed features. Substantially increases the number of confident identifications compared to search engine scores alone. Works with DDA and DIA data, and has been validated for immunopeptidomics and metaproteomics.
    
       GitHub
       Docs
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Ralf Gabriels
     Arthur Declercq

title: Tools & Software
text:
Data Standards & Utilities

  
    
      
      psm_utils
    
    A Python library for parsing, converting, and handling peptide-spectrum matches from any major search engine. Provides a unified PSM object model and format-agnostic I/O for building analysis pipelines.
    
       GitHub
       Docs
       PyPI
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Ralf Gabriels
  


  
    
      
      lesSDRF
    
    An interactive desktop app for annotating proteomics experiments in the SDRF format. Guides users through ontology-compliant metadata entry. HAMLET extends this with LLM-based pre-filling from manuscript text.
    
       GitHub
       Web app
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Sander Van Puyvelde
  


  
    
      
      Thermo Raw File Parser
    
    Converts Thermo .raw files to open formats (mzML, MGF, or Parquet) on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Widely used as a preprocessing step in proteomics pipelines.
    
       GitHub
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Robbin Bouwmeester

title: Tools & Software
text:
Taxonomy & Metaproteomics

  
    
      
      Unipept
    
    Tryptic peptide-based functional and taxonomic analysis of metaproteome samples. Exposes a public API handling millions of queries per month and integrates with major proteomics workflows via a Python and R client.
    
       GitHub
       Web app
       API docs
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Robbin Bouwmeester
     Arthur Declercq
  


  
    
      
      Peptonizer2000
    
    Provides probabilistic taxon confidence scores for metaproteomics using a Bayesian network approach. Propagates peptide-level evidence into organism-level posteriors, giving statistically grounded taxon assignments in complex microbial community samples.
    
       GitHub
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Robbin Bouwmeester

title: CompOmics
text:
Our Vision
CompOmics pushes the boundaries of computational proteomics at Ghent University and VIB. We believe that unlocking the full potential of mass spectrometry data requires tight integration of algorithmic innovation, machine learning, and open data standards.
Our mission is to develop open-source tools and community resources that make proteomics data analysis faster, more accurate, and accessible to researchers worldwide: from raw spectrum interpretation to FAIR-compliant data sharing.
We are committed to open science: all our software, datasets, and workflows are publicly available and built to last.

title: Research
text: CompOmics is a bioinformatics research group at Ghent University and VIB, focused on computational proteomics. We develop open-source software and methods to push the boundaries of mass spectrometry-based proteomics.

title: Research
text: CompOmics is a bioinformatics research group at Ghent University and VIB, focused on computational proteomics. We develop open-source software and methods to push the boundaries of mass spectrometry-based proteomics.

title: Research
text:
Research Themes

Peptide & Protein Identification
Development of database search engines, post-processing tools, and validation strategies for PSM-level and protein-level identification in DDA and DIA workflows.


AI-Based Spectrum & Retention Time Prediction
We train and apply deep learning models to predict MS2 spectra, retention times, and other peptide properties, enabling data-independent analysis and rescoring workflows.


FAIR Proteomics Data
We contribute to community standards including HUPO-PSI formats (mzML, mzTab, mzSpecLib), ProteomeXchange/PRIDE data sharing, and ProteomicsML for machine learning-ready datasets.


Proteomics Data Infrastructure
Building scalable data formats (Parquet, Feather), APIs, and pipelines to make large-scale proteomics data accessible and reusable by the community.
